dbo.name: event_log

schema.name: user_id
schema.description: Reference to the user who performed the action.

schema.name: account_id
schema.description: Reference to the company associated with the user event.

schema.name: action
schema.description: A description of the action performed by the user (e.g., 'login', 'created_invoice', 'updated_profile').

schema.name: metadata
schema.description: Additional data related to the event, such as resource IDs, old/new values, or other contextual information.

dbo.description: Stores logs of user activities and events within the application.

schema.name: owner_user
schema.description: The user who owns this conversation thread.

schema.name: title
schema.description: The title or subject of the conversation.

schema.name: last_message_at
schema.description: Timestamp of the last message in the conversation.

dbo.name: agent_message

schema.name: conversation
schema.description: Reference to the parent conversation thread.

schema.name: role
schema.description: The role of the message sender (e.g., system, user, assistant, tool).

schema.name: content
schema.description: The content of the message, compatible with AI SDK.

dbo.description: Stores individual messages within conversation threads.

run.name: mvp:template_string

input.name: template
input.value:
Xano Backend Demo

Demo App

A demo built on Xano with out-of-the-box authentication endpoints and an example Agent that lets you interact with a chatbot powered by Xano Docs.

ð¬ Video: Learn how to use this Demo App

Demo Overview

Signup: Create new accounts through your Xano signup endpoint with secure password hashing.
Login: Authenticate users via POST request to your Xano login endpoint with email/password credentials.
Password Reset: Trigger email-based password recovery using your Xano reset endpoint.
Agent Chatbot: Interact with an example chatbot connected to an MCP with Xano Docs using Xano Agents.

Login â Technical Implementation

Step 1: Input validation
Step 2: POST to /auth/login
Step 3: Generate Auth Token
Step 4: Redirect to Agent chat
i
When you're ready to remove the demo, delete the API endpoint GET /1_start_here_demo_page from your workspace.

Log in

Access your account

API Integration
Endpoint:
Request Body: { email, password }
Response: Authentication token

This form connects directly to your Xano authentication system for secure user login.

Use at least 8 characters.

Create account Forgot password?

Xano Backend Demo

Demo App

A demo built on Xano with out-of-the-box authentication endpoints and an example Agent that lets you interact with a chatbot powered by Xano Docs.

ð¬ Video: Learn how to use this Demo App

Demo Overview

Signup: Create new accounts through your Xano signup endpoint with secure password hashing.
Login: Authenticate users via POST request to your Xano login endpoint with email/password credentials.
Password Reset: Trigger email-based password recovery using your Xano reset endpoint.
Agent Chatbot: Interact with an example chatbot connected to an MCP with Xano Docs using Xano Agents.

Password Reset â Technical Implementation

Step 1: Email validation against Xano user database
Step 2: Generate secure, time-limited reset token
Step 3: Send email with reset link via your email service
Step 4: User clicks link â password reset form â update in Xano
i
When you're ready to remove the demo, delete the API endpoint GET /1_start_here_demo_page from your workspace.

Request reset link

Enter your email

API Integration
Endpoint: ?email=you@example.com
Query Param: email
Response: Message confirming a reset link (if the email exists)

This form targets your Xano reset endpoint; authentication is not required.

Notice: The Send Email function only sends to the instance owner's Email until you provide your own API key and settings. See the function in Xano for more info.

Back to login
Xano Backend Demo

Demo App

A demo built on Xano with out-of-the-box authentication endpoints and an example Agent that lets you interact with a chatbot powered by Xano Docs.

ð¬ Video: Learn how to use this Demo App

Demo Overview

Signup: Create new accounts through your Xano signup endpoint with secure password hashing.
Login: Authenticate users via POST request to your Xano login endpoint with email/password credentials.
Password Reset: Trigger email-based password recovery using your Xano reset endpoint.
Agent Chatbot: Interact with an example chatbot connected to an MCP with Xano Docs using Xano Agents.

Update Password â Technical Implementation

1) Read magic_token and email from URL
2) POST /reset/magic-link-login â store authToken
3) POST /reset/update_password with Bearer token
4) On success, show confirmation & âContinue to appâ
i
When you're ready to remove the demo, delete the API endpoint GET /1_start_here_demo_page from your workspace.

Update password

Checking your reset linkâ¦

API Integration
Magic-link login: (body: { magic_token, email? })
Password update: (body: { password, confirm_password }, Bearer auth)
Email:
Back to login

Password successfully saved

You can now continue to the app.

Xano Example Agent - Chat UI
API
Method: POST
Base URL:
URL:
Headers
Authorization: Bearer
Content-Type: application/json
Parameters
  • message â array of objects
  • conversation_id â integer (optional)
Sample message JSON

          

            
          

        

      

    


    
    

      

        
        

          
Xano Example Agent Chatbot

          

            

              
Now that youâve signed in using Xanoâs authentication endpoints, welcome to the demo UI for interacting with the Xano Example Agent inside your instance.

              
As an example, this Agent includes tools connected to an MCP Server of Xano documentation â allowing it to answer your questions about Xano directly from the docs.

            

            

              
How to Explore

              
    
                
  • âTo use the Agent in your own workspace, navigate to AI > Agents in Xano.
    • 
                
  • âTo view the connected tools (e.g., search_xano_docs), open the Xano Example Agent in your workspace.
    • 
                
  • âType a message below and start interacting with the Agent chatbot.
    •

query.name: 1_start_here_demo_page
query.description: This endpoint provides a demo UI when pasted in your browser so you can interact with your Xano APIs for authentication, password reset, and chatbot conversation. It helps demonstrate how a backend interacts with a front-end to create a full app experience. This page is just a demo page that is meant to be removed later, not edited and built upon.

tool.name: search_xano_docs
tool.description: This tool connects to the Mintlify MCP Server, which accesses and searches the Xano Documentation based on a search query.
tool.instructions: This allows you to search the Xano Documentation and input a search query. It will return a pages of the Xano Documentation based on the search query. This can be used to answer and analyze questions around Xano and how to use it.

toolset.name: Xano Example Agent
toolset.description:
The Xano Example Agent gets you started with a basic AI Agent to integrate into your workflows. You can easily build on top of it, connect tools, and adjust the prompt to adapt to your use case.

It comes with a Tool connected to the Xano Documentation, which has context to the Xano Docs. To test the Baseline Agent, open the Demo API in the Authentication API Group.

agent_settings.system_prompt:
You are a helpful and versatile AI assistant. Your core responsibility is to understand and respond to user inquiries by providing accurate, clear, and concise information or completing general tasks as requested.

You rely solely on your general knowledge and reasoning abilities.

Tools available:
-You can Xano documentation with the tool SearchXanoDocs. This access the pages of the Xano documentation, which can be used to answer questions based on the search query.

When responding:
- Always strive to be helpful and provide accurate information.
- If a task requires external information or capabilities you do not possess, clearly state your limitations.
- Explain your reasoning or thought process when appropriate, especially for complex inquiries.
- Provide clear and easy-to-understand responses.

### Dynamic Context
You will receive the user's query or message as an argument.

### Output Expectations
- Responses should be clear, concise, and directly address the user's query.
- Output should be in a readable Markdown format if structuring information or providing lists.
- Ensure the response completely answers the user's request based on the information available to you.

workspace.name: Battery Asset Intelligence Platform
workspace.description: The Battery Asset Intelligence Infrastructure Layer is a comprehensive platform designed to provide detailed tracking and intelligence for Electric Vehicle (EV) batteries, aligning with EU Battery Passport specifications. It integrates a wide array of data including chemistry, manufacturing batches, cycle history, State of Health (SoH), repair/failure history, and real-time operational data. The platform leverages AI/ML for advanced analytics such as residual value modeling and degradation forecasting. It offers a robust API layer for critical functionalities like battery health, residual value, risk scoring, lifecycle certification, and second-life viability. Serving an application layer for users, banks, lenders, auctions, fleet operators, and recyclers, the platform utilizes FlutterFlow for its frontend dashboards and portals. A strong emphasis is placed on security and compliance, ensuring SOC2 readiness, AES-256 data encryption at rest, TLS 1.3, Role-Based Access Control (RBAC), multi-tenant isolation, and GDPR-ready design.

checklist_tasks.title: Populate your Battery data
checklist_tasks.description: Navigate to your `battery` database table and add some initial battery records with relevant details like chemistry, manufacturing batch, and current State of Health (SoH).

checklist_tasks.title: Test the Battery CRUD API
checklist_tasks.description: Go to the `BatteryAssetIntelligenceAPI` group, find the `Add Battery` (POST /battery) endpoint, and test it in Xano to create a new battery entry programmatically.

checklist_tasks.title: Connect your FlutterFlow Frontend
checklist_tasks.description: Connect your FlutterFlow application to the `BatteryAssetIntelligenceAPI` endpoints, starting with fetching a list of batteries or organizations.

checklist_tasks.title: Implement Battery Health API
checklist_tasks.description: Create a new API endpoint in the `BatteryAssetIntelligenceAPI` group that calculates and returns a comprehensive battery health report based on historical data and current SoH.
